item.title: I expect this to die in user sub but I have to get this off my chest.
item.description:
I'm a very quite guy and have a hard time showing certain emotions.
I sux at any signs/signals from any girl
Yeah I texted her something really weird from a horror movie I liked and was scared I blew it before I could even get friend zoned
I really sux at telling if a girl is just being nice or flirting with me.
I'm ashamed that she had to ask me if I was planing to ask her out and I'm scared
I'm terrified that I'll mess up my chances with this awesome girl but it's not going to stop me from trying. But I have no idea where to take her for our 1st date. Edit: thanks to everyone for all the great advice and encouragement, it really helps. Definitely have some stuff to think of. Even though we have been talking to each other for 2-3 months now, we've seen and had some small talk for about a year. PSN= Playstation network. She found out i had a playstation and was the one to ask for my ID and start texting to me on PSN and we've been constantly texting each other. Also she gave me her number but we still text each other through PSN and we have been talking on the phone every once and a while. Also I should clarify that we are not co-workers. We work for different companies that happen to be in the same building(think like different stores in same mall, kind of thing) so it's my fault for saying I met her at work, i didnt actually mean that we worked together.

item.title: I am shookith
item.description: All my delivery information is correct. I checked it a thousand times. My groceries were getting delivered to my across the street neighbors. I say, hey guy! those may be my groceries. Guy says, no way, impossible, he is 100% this is the correct address. I think, ok, this guy OBVIOUSLY knows what he's talking about. I get the above text*your groceries have been delivered* oh no! this is my neighbor's front porch. No biggy- let me just go grab them. Go put my shoes on, go back outside, AND groceries vanished. I knock on her door. THIS BITCH LOCKS THE DOOR!!!!! and her three year old is looking out the window and she's like, shhh shhh shhhh get down. WHAT?!?! umm ok. So I go inside and think wtf am I to do? I'll leave a note. Maybe they're just anti social, I mean, I never answer my door. So the note basically says, hey I'm your neighbor, I think my groceries got delivered to your house by mistake. Please call me and I'll grab them. Nothing. I go back a second time and knock, and again I hear the mom telling her kid to keep quiet.... Jesus Christ. Umm ok.. The groceries are a lost cause. Whatever. It sucks, but I got a refund... So all in all no harm done. But I cannot get this off my mind... Like THIS NEIGHBOR BITCH stole my fucking groceries. She doesn't know my situation. What if that was the last of my money? What if I couldn't get a refund? What if I now have no food until next paycheck? And mind you, this lady rents a jumpy castle for her kid every other month. And we live in a lower class neighborhood... And she has bouncy castle money, and if I didn't get the refund, I would be in trouble. Anyways...I know I should just let it go, but I feel it in my bones that I must get my revenge. And what?!? Now they're gonna dodge me for the next forever??? They live across the fucking street.. Idk. Any advice? Also I just wanted to vent... Thank you. Tldr: my neighbors stole my groceries and I am perturbed.
item.dc:creator: izzi7713

item.dc:creator: m4rjinn

item.title: You’re only a lost cause when you fail to change
item.description:
So I grew up as a result of a piss poor divorce. I lived with my mom and my dad had her car repoed and our house foreclosed upon. My mom grinded to survive and had health problems. So I never really had anyone watching out or directing me. I never studied or did homework in high school but still passed classes. Personally i think its because i amused my teachers with my style of thinking.
Never had a teacher take a real interest in helping me so i never took the SATs or any standardized test. I never knew when to apply for college. I worked a full time job in high school and partied a lot with the other poor kids that drank to feel like things werent completely shitty. Truly had some great times but they were leading nowhere.
Fell into the belief that if you worked hard you could rise through the ranks and make enough money to live comfortably. Retail isnt like that though. Even if you move up the money isnt there until you reach district level and district level requires a degree. I didnt realize this for too long.
I bounced around a bit but ultimately its damn near impossible to support yourself on retail salary. I had to move back with my mom in a small town in a state that i hated. I worked a retail job for a year or so. I hated everything about my life. Seeing my old friends graduating college sucked bad. They were moving on and i wasnt. One day my boss told me he wanted me to put in for an assistant manager position. The last thing i wanted was that. That would cement my life as it was and i hated my life. So i took a stick of dynamite and blew up my life. I quit shortly after i was told to apply for the job.
Yup fastest way out of that life joining the military. I was the old guy at basic. I had no education so i had no increased rank but i had a leg up knowing how shitty life outside was for the uninitiated, but i was the initiated. Wage slavery sucked and soon i would find out being the outsider in the military would also suck. To old to hang out with people my rank. Too low rank to hang out with people my age. I was on a weird island in the military. I enjoyed my job but was constantly told if i effed up i would go to jail. I hated that weight. I hated feeling like i was gonna go to jail because i made a mistake when i was tired or whatever.
Never had a lot of friends so i had time. The military also has this amazing thing where they pay for education while youre in. Without friends to hang out with, i had a lot and i mean a lot of time to utilize this benefit. I used every cent they would give me to get my associates and star in on my bachelors. Before i got out i had an associates and halfish of my bachelors.
First chance i got i said the hell with this and booked it out of there. Dont get me wrong the military gave me great experiences and great skills, i just didnt want to stay for more of the crappy times. There seemed to be plenty of those. When getting out i saw guys that didnt use their benefits like i had. They just wanted to go back to their home towns and work for the local construction company. Thats fine for some people but i hated where i would have gone so i refused to do that.
Once i got out i refused to move to my “home of record”. Instead i moved across county to take classes in person. I was again the old guy but didnt care that time. I had scraped and clawed my way to this point. I was really happy i hadnt gone straight to college because getting slapped by reality made me appreciate school more.
This part sucked. I really couldn’t wait for in person classes. I finally got it and then the second semester back i got rocked with covid lock downs and was forced into online learning again. It was so hard to keep going during this time but what else was i gonna do? I somehow survived and moved forward towards my B.S.
In the middle of covid i was able to apply to grad school for right after i was done with my bachelors. Looking back at it, i think its strange that I barely showed up to high school and now i was voluntarily signing up for more school. Amazingly though that was what was happening.
Surprisingly my mentality about nothing being black and white right and wrong paid off in grad school. I did better in grad school than i had ever done in any other schooling ever. Riding that high, i went and talked to a professor. Turns out my professor had connections at the place i wanted to work and got me a job just because i reached out.
I just graduated with my masters. I cannot tell you how different my life is. I make more money than anyone in my family. I have more education. I have more happiness and security too. The kid i was in high school would not recognize who i am now and that makes me kind of happy. I cant tell you how important those shitty experiences i had earlier in my life were to me getting to this point. I am grateful for the bad time and i am grateful to those who overlooked me. Without all of it i would not be here.
To those of you in shit situations. You’re not done. You’re not over. You can prevail. If my dumbass can do it, you defiantly can. Sometimes you have to blow shit up to build things from the ground up. Its scary and hard but the alternative of staying stagnant is far worse.

item.title: I'm honestly not sure how to handle this.
item.description:
I've made it a personal policy to ignore drama and help where I can. My brother isn't the greatest person, but he's trying. Problem is, he's got a poor taste in company, and it's not uncommon for him to bring his girlfriend around from time to time. Today one of their arguments became really loud...
I became involved, separated the two, and kept things as close to peaceful as possible. It was decided then that she wouldn't be coming back after this. Having some experience in security work, I recognized that the two would just provoke eachother, but lordy lordry, the GF kept trying to yell all manners of insults and criticisms in an attempt to rile up my brother.
I remained courteous, ensured that the GF had time enough to gather her things. What bothered me however was that somewhere between her loud grievances, she mentioned calling someone to "make sure he gets what he's got coming to him" and that it would probably be her father or uncle. I was dismissive of it at first, but noted it.
The more distance you can get between two parties with hostile attitudes, the better. She wanted to call for a ride home, and I wanted as much of a buffer zone as I could create. The problem came up when she began telling me directly that she'd call someone, and that "He'd get what's coming to him, and you for enabling him."
She called someone else, started describing in detail her opinions of myself and my brother, suggested I was part of the problem, and then stepped out of earshot. I used to work security for a bit, so I decided that maybe I should listen to my gut and quietly text the police about the safety concern.
The police arrived, two vehicles. They were pretty quick to hear out my concerns.
I was immediately thankful for the police presence when these strangers pulled up, and exited their vehicle. Not one or two, but THREE people exited the vehicle and approached quickly. The police officer interviewing the GF instructed all three to immediately return to their vehicle.
I've seen police work quite a bit in my old job. Usually when someone starts making threats, it's easy to trespass them and ensure they never come back. Three people pulling up and charging in the direction of the police? Icing on the cake.
Things took a pretty quick turn-around after the GF talked with the police. The responding officers decided that it was a domestic abuse incident, and instead cuffed my brother then and there. Alright, that's something that can be addressed in court.
Professionalism and courtesy are key, but I did notice that the police were focusing unusually on only one issue, while neglecting to mention anything about the original problem. When I asked them if they'd at least gathered any information, in case the group returned, all three said no. I was dumbfounded. Okay, there's some requirements to request an ID, but that doesn't stop the common sense practice of getting a license plate, or any sort of information that could be referenced if the trio or GF returned at a later time. Instead they insisted that "Nothing happened".
I pointed out the unusual logic they were employing, specifically that they themselves witnessed a vehicle pull up with three occupants that were not hesitant to approach with haste. Their response was that the group came to pick her up, as she'd called for a ride home. It wasn't hard to appreciate the strangeness of it. A person threatens to call someone and have said someone make sure my brother "gets what he has coming", and then three people show up when one could have easily come to get her, and then ALL THREE make a hasty advance until they recognize the presence of law enforcement... ...but no, nothing suspicious there, apparently. I think what made it sting a little more was that the officer suggested the additional numbers were needed because "they were concerned". This was coming from a police officer. A police officer. The a member of the law enforcement agency I called because I was concerned.
This one really got me. While they were interviewing her, she apparently told them she was a resident of the property. No keys, not here for any extended time, but the police took her at her word. Sucks for me, because they refused to budge on this even a little.
This hurt. In security work, we don't have room for questions like this to go unanswered. Either we answer it with facts, or our input is treated as little more than a poorly timed fart during a hurricane.
TLDR: Called the police to be present if anyone showed up to be violent, police stop a group that showed up to be violent, then arrested my brother and proceeded to let the group go without identifying any of them in any fashion that would allow a followup if they show up to be violent again. I don't care about upvotes. I just want help. I'm concerned for my safety, and the police only made things worse without actually addressing the problem.
item.dc:creator: WriteorWrong

item.title: Reaction dump part 1
item.description: Stolen reactions
item.dc:creator: Error112

item.title: Gravestone Safety 101: DON’T.
item.description:
Recap: I clear out overgrown graves in my local cemetery. Link: https://imgur.com/gallery/oJCrtWq . I’ve gotten many people asking me about what tools I use, what cleaning chemicals they should use, etc… I am addressing that in particular. This is actually information that I have posted before, but I wanted to compile it into an album dedicated to gravestone safety. The other one was just in an album about my graveyard adventures. Link: https://imgur.com/gallery/S8QxYZk . Now all of this info is in a post dedicated to gravestone safety!
I worked for the horticulture department at Texas State University for an entire year. My job centered around garden maintenance. I relocated saplings, lightly washed off decorative stones, and helped maintain rose vines that curled around a gazebo on campus. I am trained in garden maintenance and “garden restoration,” as I call it. I am 100% confident in my ability to get the soil off of an overgrown headstone. I can also gently wash off the fresh dirt/mud. . BUT: I DO NOT CLEAN THE HEADSTONES, BECAUSE I LACK TRAINING. . Headstone restoration is a matter of historical preservation, and needs a trained and educated person. That goes beyond my ability. I don’t ever go into the graveyard with the intent to clean the headstone. I just restore the garden around it.
This is all I’m doing in the graveyard. I restore gardens, not gravestones!
You might **think** it looks better when it’s scrubbed down, but it’s been made weaker - which ruins its historical preservation.
HOLD UP!! HOLD UP!! HOLD UP!!
Keep in mind that this is an overly simplified version of biology. Because the lichen are munching on the stone, they basically have their little fungal/algae teeth sunk into it. Try to imagine putting a piece of steak in your mouth and clenching down on it. When someone tries to pry the steak from your teeth, it tears. This is what will happen if you try to brush lichen off of a stone. You won’t SEE the damage, but it’s there, and it will weaken the stone (which is not good for historical preservation). You can read more about lichen here! Link: https://lisbdnet.com/how-does-lichen-break-down-rock/
Think of it like this: Your unprotected smart phone falls onto the floor. It cracks. The more it falls onto the ground, the bigger those cracks will grow, and pieces of the screen might come off entirely. An old and heavy gravestone isn’t going to sit upright until it’s re-welded onto it. The more you try to prop it up, the more it will fall down, and like a smartphone, any other cracks and damage it has sustained are going to get bigger.
I know you mean well, and it’s awesome that you want to put some loving care into something old and exposed to the elements. But cleaning a gravestone is a historical preservation procedure. Most city graveyard committees will have volunteer opportunities where you can get real training in handling a delicate headstone. But until then, the best thing you can do for an old gravestone is to do NOTHING to the stone! But weeding around it isn’t going to hurt it. As long as you DON’T TOUCH THE STONE!!
Don’t let this discourage you, though. You can have an awesome time cleaning headstones as soon as you get permission and real training in it! Happy graveyard exploration! :)

item.title: Dump, pox edition
item.description:
After two years of making good choices
Masking, distancing, avoiding crowded places
The pox has hit my house... the kids missed the last fun days of school
Spouse and I had to take days off work.
We're all vaccinated and boosted as appropriate
But it's still hitting hard.
Thankfully the youngest is starting to perk up, so that means it must come to an end.
All of that anxiety any fear had dimmed until the positive tests came back.
I felt the original fear again, from the early days, what if one of us has a really bad case?
All I can do is keep everyone hydrated, resting, taking vitamins.
We're re-watching Stranger Things before the new season drops. It's a good distraction from the coughing.
We have friends and family, food and supplies, and flexible virtual jobs.
Considering all of that I guess getting the pox now is best case scenario, rather than in the beginning with no vaccine and hospitals full.
We'll stay home and quietly quarantine, like so many other families.
I just kind of wanted to call into the void a bit.
Hopefully this means we'll be extra immune for the next few months.
The dog is, of course, delighted that we're all home and being lazy together. Thanks to anyone who read my random musings, may your homes be healthy.
item.dc:creator: mynameisminerva
